perturbation.original_text: 1.14 "Product(s)" means the Extract and Custom Reagents.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.14 "Product(s)" means the Extract. Custom Reagents are excluded.
perturbation.explanation: This change alters the definition of "Product(s)". Initially, it includes both Extract and Custom Reagents. Now, Custom Reagents are explicitly excluded. This will contradict later sections that assume 'Product(s)' includes both, particularly concerning manufacturing and quality standards. This affects the scope of supply obligations for Custom Reagents and creates uncertainty regarding quality control measures that should be applied to them, specifically in section 2.16 where "Other Extracts" are deemed Extract for purpose of some sections of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: From time-to-time, subject to an agreed-upon Work Order, SutroVax may place purchase orders for quantities of research grade extract derived from strains of E. Coli other than that set forth on Schedule 1 attached hereto (each an "Other Extract"). Sutro shall use Commercially Reasonable Efforts to accept such purchase orders (and shall accept such purchase orders placed consistent with an agreed-upon Work Order) and manufacture and supply to SutroVax such Other Extracts. For the purpose of calculating the Price for Other Extracts in accordance with this Section, the Price will be the cost of materials and Sutro's labor at an FTE rate of [***] per year for Sutro laboratory scientists. Upon Sutro's acceptance of a purchase order for Other Extract, such Other Extract in such purchase order shall be deemed Extract for purposes of Sections 2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.4.1 (solely with respect to the last two sentences thereof), 2.5, 2.6, 2.7, 2.8, 2.9.1 , 3.1, 4, 5, 6, 7 and 9.
perturbation.justification: The change to section 1.14 explicitly excludes 'Custom Reagents' from the definition of 'Product(s)'. The contradicted text refers to 'Other Extracts' being deemed 'Extract' (and therefore a Product per the original definition) for the purposes of Sections 2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.4.1, 2.5, 2.6, 2.7, 2.8, 2.9.1, 3.1, 4, 5, 6, 7 and 9. If 'Custom Reagents' are not considered 'Products' and these reagents are deemed as Other Extracts, then the referenced clauses are logically impacted. For instance, the manufacturing standards that apply to 'Products' might now arguably not apply to the 'Other Extracts' if they are determined to be 'Custom Reagents', creating a clear conflict in how the quality control obligations are to be interpreted.

perturbation.original_text: 1.20 "Specifications" means, with respect to a Product or applicable Component thereof, all written product, regulatory, Manufacturing, release criterion, quality control and quality assurance procedures, processes, practices, standards, instructions and specifications applicable to the Manufacture of such Product or Component, as agreed to by the Parties in writing from time to time. The initial Specifications for the Products are attached hereto as Schedule 2.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.20 "Specifications" means, with respect to a Product, all written product, regulatory, Manufacturing, release criterion, quality control and quality assurance procedures, processes, practices, standards, instructions and specifications applicable to the Manufacture of such Product, as agreed to by the Parties in writing from time to time. The initial Specifications for the Products are attached hereto as Schedule 2. For clarity, Specifications do not include regulatory guidelines.
perturbation.explanation: The modification limits "Specifications" to exclude 'regulatory guidelines', which were previously implicitly included. This creates conflict when determining 'Required Standards' in section 1.19. The definition of Specifications excluding regulatory guidelines will create inconsistencies regarding compliance expectations in the definition of Required Standards and therefore the manufacturing obligations
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.19 "Required Standards" means Applicable Law, the Specifications, and the warranties given by Sutro in Section 7.3, provided that Required Standards shall not include compliance with cGMPs for Custom Reagents, Extract identified as "research grade" Extract or Other Extracts.
perturbation.justification: The modification to the definition of "Specifications" explicitly excludes 'regulatory guidelines', which were implicitly included in the original definition. The definition of "Required Standards" in section 1.19 includes "the Specifications". By excluding regulatory guidelines from the definition of 'Specifications', the modified text creates a contradiction because "Required Standards" now no longer includes regulatory guidelines through the definition of specifications, where previously it would have.

perturbation.original_text: 2.6 Delivery and Risk of Loss. Sutro shall mark Product for delivery to the destination(s) specified by SutroVax. All shipments of Product(s) shall be delivered [***] (lncoterms 2010) Sutro Facility. Title and risk of loss and damage to the Product(s) shall remain with Sutro until the Product(s) are delivered in accordance with the foregoing, at which time title and risk ofloss and damage to the Product(s) shall pass to SutroVax. SutroVax will arrange for shipping from Sutro's Facility to the destination specified by SutroVax at SutroVax's cost and expense.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.6 Delivery and Risk of Loss. Sutro shall mark Product for delivery to the destination(s) specified by SutroVax. All shipments of Extracts shall be delivered [***] (lncoterms 2010) Sutro Facility, while Custom Reagents will be delivered digitally via secure file transfer protocol. Title and risk of loss and damage to the Extracts shall remain with Sutro until the Extracts are delivered in accordance with the foregoing, at which time title and risk ofloss and damage to the Extracts shall pass to SutroVax.  For Custom Reagents, title and risk of loss passes to SutroVax once the digital file is uploaded to the secure server. SutroVax will arrange for shipping from Sutro's Facility to the destination specified by SutroVax at SutroVax's cost and expense.
perturbation.explanation: This alteration introduces different delivery methods and risk transfer points for Extracts versus Custom Reagents. Extracts are physically shipped, whereas Custom Reagents are delivered digitally. This creates a contradiction with sections that discuss 'shipments of Product(s)' without differentiating between the two, specifically the testing procedures described in Article 4. The in-text contradiction arises from the use of "shipment of Product(s)" without considering that Custom Reagents are not physically shipped anymore.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 Product Testing and Inspections. Each shipment of Product shall be accompanied by a certificate of analysis describing all current requirements of the Specifications and results of tests performed on such Product and a certificate of conformity certifying that the quantities of Product supplied have been Manufactured, controlled and released according to the Required Standards ("COA/COC") as set forth in the applicable Quality Agreement (subject to SutroVax's conduct of the SutroVax Activity Test to confirm Extract meets the SutroVax Activity Criteria). The COA/COC acceptance criteria for each Product shall be set forth in the Specification for such Product. Two of the tests and corresponding COA/COC acceptance criteria for the Extract shall be the performance of a productivity (Activity) test of the applicable Sutro protein (the "Sutro Activity Test" and "Sutro Activity Criteria") and the performance of a productivity (activity) test of the applicable Vaccine Composition (the "SutroVax Activity Test" and "SutroVax Activity Criteria"). Sutro shall perform the Sutro Activity Test to confirm that all shipments of Extract meet the Sutro Activity Criteria and SutroVax (or its designee) shall perform the SutroVax Activity Test to confirm that all shipments of Extract meet the SutroVax Activity Criteria. Sutro will also provide SutroVax with Material Safety Data Sheets ("MSDS") or an equivalent instrument recognized by the applicable Regulatory Authority as required for the Product(s), and updates of the same as necessary.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 2.6 introduces different delivery methods for 'Extracts' and 'Custom Reagents' (physical shipment vs. digital transfer). This directly contradicts Section 4.1, which refers to 'Each shipment of Product' needing a certificate of analysis (COA/COC) and testing procedures. Since Custom Reagents are no longer physically shipped, the requirement of a COA/COC accompanying 'each shipment of Product' becomes inapplicable and contradictory for Custom Reagents, thus impacting enforceability and creating internal inconsistency.
